UQoL Indicators in Barcelona 2026 - PCA

1. Scope of analysis

The following libraries are loaded to support data manipulation, statistical analysis, and visualisation throughout this notebook. All packages are installed automatically if not already available in the R environment.

# Library to open .sas files
if (!require("haven")) install.packages("haven")
G3;Cargando paquete requerido: haven
g
library(haven)

# Library for contingency tables and correlation searches
if (!require("dplyr")) install.packages("dplyr")
G3;Cargando paquete requerido: dplyr
gG3;
Adjuntando el paquete: ‘dplyr’

gG3;The following objects are masked from ‘package:stats’:

    filter, lag

gG3;The following objects are masked from ‘package:base’:

    intersect, setdiff, setequal, union

g
library(dplyr)

if (!require("readxl")) install.packages("readxl")
G3;Cargando paquete requerido: readxl
g
library(readxl)

if (!require("scales")) install.packages("scales")
G3;Cargando paquete requerido: scales
g
library(scales)

# Libraries for MCA analysis
if (!require("FactoMineR")) install.packages("FactoMineR")
G3;Cargando paquete requerido: FactoMineR

  print.htmlwidget tools:rstudio
g

library(factoextra)

library(openxlsx)

# Libraries for graphics and export
if (!require("ggplot2")) install.packages("ggplot2")
library(ggplot2)
if (!require("ggpubr")) install.packages("ggpubr")

if (!require("here")) install.packages("here")
G3;Cargando paquete requerido: here
gG2;H2;Avisoh: package ‘here’ was built under R version 4.4.3g
G3;here() starts at C:/Users/aurel/Dropbox/1. Doctorado/7. TESIS/Chapter 3/Urban Quality of Life Barcelona/UQoL_Barcelona
g
library(here)

The analytical workflow presented in this chapter was implemented in R using the RStudio environment and is documented as an annotated R Notebook. All data pre-processing steps — including format standardisation, polarity correction, and the exclusion of neutral indicators — are recorded sequentially, followed by the Principal Component Analysis computations, indicator weighting procedures, and composite index aggregation for each UQoL dimension. Outputs, including correlation matrices, Scree plots, and weighted scores, are generated inline within the notebook. The complete, annotated code is included in Appendix to ensure the full reproducibility of the results presented in this chapter.

Output directories are created here so that all results are saved in an organised structure relative to the location of this notebook. Each dimension has its own subdirectory within a parent Results folder.

dirs <- c(
  here("1_PCA", "Results", "Economic"),
  here("1_PCA", "Results", "Healthcare"),
  here("1_PCA", "Results", "Housing"),
  here("1_PCA", "Results", "Infrastructures"),
  here("1_PCA", "Results", "Mobility"),
  here("1_PCA", "Results", "Social"),
  here("1_PCA", "Results", "Urban"),
  here("1_PCA", "Results", "Aggregated")
)
for (d in dirs) dir.create(d, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

The raw dataset is loaded from the same directory as this notebook. The file contains one row per neighbourhood and one column per indicator.

indicators_raw <- read.csv(
  here("1_PCA", "Indicators PCA_Neighbourhood data.csv"),
  header = TRUE,
  sep = ";"
)

The first rows of the dataset are displayed below as a preview to confirm the file has been loaded correctly.

The total number of neighbourhoods (rows) and indicators (columns) is printed below.

The file size is  73 cases (neighbourhoods 
, and  52 indicators

2. Data preprocessing

Because of the data format, a series of transformations are necessary to prepare them for analysis.

2.1 Transform type

The satisfaction variable is renamed to a standardised label for consistency across the analysis.

names(indicators_raw)[names(indicators_raw) == "Satisfacción_Barrio"] <- "satisfaction"

The full list of column names is printed to verify the structure of the dataset.

colnames(indicators_raw)

All indicator columns are converted to numeric format to allow aggregation and work. In the original data, decimal values are recorded with a comma separator. These are replaced with a decimal point to allow calculations. Also, whitespace is also removed. This step is required before any calculations can be performed.

# Replace commas with decimal points and remove whitespace in all relevant columns
cols_to_convert <- c("X805_Eco_Individual.income", "X852_Eco_Individual.income","X582_Eco_Occupation",
"X822_Eco_Occupation","X1014_Eco_Occupation","X283_Eco_Occupation",
"X43_Eco_Tourism.development", "X279_Eco_", "X523_Eco_", "X877_Inf_Air.quality", "X671_Inf_Education", "X809_Inf_Education","X744_Inf_Education", "X974_Inf_Education", 
"X10_Healthcare", "X642_Healthcare", "X257_Healthcare", "X902_Healthcare", "X148_Housing", "X465_Housing", "X674_Housing", "X477_Housing", "X139_Housing","X866_Housing", "X653_Housing", "X569_Inf_Leisure.and.cultural.activities",
"X198_Inf_Leisure.and.cultural.activities", "X484_Inf_Nature.based.assets", 
"X633_Inf_Nature.based.assets", "X479_Inf_Noise", "X1041_Inf_Security.and.safety",
"X30_Inf_Water.consumption", "X989_Mob_", "X361_Mob_", "X515_Mob_",
"X9_Soc_Cultural.diversity.and.identity", "X329_Soc_Cultural.diversity.and.identity", 
"X670_Soc_Cultural.diversity.and.identity", "X123_Soc_Demographics", "X589_Soc_Density.of.social.relations", "X331_Soc_Equality.and.diversity",
"X799_Soc_Public.participation.and.trust.in.institutions", "X565_Soc_Social.participation","X348_Urb_Population.density", "X265_Urb_", "X534_Urb_", "satisfaction")

# Apply transformation to all columns in a loop
for (col in cols_to_convert) {
  indicators_raw[[col]] <- gsub(",", ".", indicators_raw[[col]]) 
  # Remove whitespaces
 indicators_raw[[col]] <- gsub("\\s+", "", indicators_raw[[col]])
  #Convert to numerical
  indicators_raw[[col]] <- as.numeric(indicators_raw[[col]])
}

# Verify conversion
summary(indicators_raw[cols_to_convert])

2.2 Polarity revision

Each indicator has a defined polarity: positive indicators increase in value as the UQoL dimension improves; negative indicators decrease. To ensure all indicators point in the same direction, such as higher values meaning better conditions, the sign of negative indicators is multiplied by −1. The function below defines which indicators require inversion. The polarity indications are the result of expert consultation.

# Function to invert signs of negative indicators
invert_negative_indicators <- function(df) {
  columns_to_invert <- c(
    "X852_Eco_Individual.income",
    "X822_Eco_Occupation",
    "X1014_Eco_Occupation",
    "X283_Eco_Occupation",
    "X43_Eco_Tourism.development",
    "X674_Housing",
    "X866_Housing",
    "X139_Housing",
    "X809_Inf_Education",
    "X974_Inf_Education",
    "X361_Mob_",
    "X989_Mob_",
    "X799_Soc_Public.participation.and.trust.in.institutions",
    "X479_Inf_Noise",
    "X348_Urb_Population.density"
  )

  for (col in columns_to_invert) {
    if (col %in% colnames(df)) {
      df[[col]] <- -1 * df[[col]]
    }
  }

  return(df)
}

The function is applied to the full dataset, producing a new object in which all negative indicators have been inverted.

inverted_indicators <- invert_negative_indicators(indicators_raw)

Neutral indicators, meaning those that cannot be considered as either improving or worsening, or either indications of improvement or worsen of UQoL, are excluded from all subsequent analyses. This step removes them from the dataset in a single operation so that no dimension subset need handle exclusions individually. Seven indicators are removed at this stage (see Appendix for the full polarity classification).

neutral_indicators <- c(
  "X148_Housing",
  "X30_Inf_Water.consumption",
  "X9_Soc_Cultural.diversity.and.identity",
  "X670_Soc_Cultural.diversity.and.identity",
  "X123_Soc_Demographics",
  "X589_Soc_Density.of.social.relations",
  "X331_Soc_Equality.and.diversity"
)
#Save the new dataset without the neutral indicators so they don't affect results
inverted_indicators <- inverted_indicators[,
  !colnames(inverted_indicators) %in% neutral_indicators
]

2.3 Plotting format setting

The colour scheme used in all correlation matrices and Scree plots is defined here centrally for consistency.

colors = c("#FF5050", "#E7DFC6", "#64F58D")
col = "#00FDDC"   # For highlighted PC points and lines

3. PCA

For each UQoL dimension, the following sequence is applied:

  1. a subset of the relevant indicators is extracted.
  2. a correlation matrix is computed and visualised to check for multicollinearity.
  3. a Principal Component Analysis (PCA) is run.
  4. the variance explained by each component is calculated and displayed as a Scree plot.
  5. indicator weights are derived from the component loadings and normalised to sum to one.

3.1 Economic Dimension

The economic dimension subset includes nine indicators covering individual income, occupation, and tourism development.

  economic_indicators <- inverted_indicators[, c( "X805_Eco_Individual.income",
"X852_Eco_Individual.income",
"X582_Eco_Occupation","X822_Eco_Occupation",
"X1014_Eco_Occupation","X283_Eco_Occupation",
"X43_Eco_Tourism.development",
"X279_Eco_","X523_Eco_"
)]

summary(economic_indicators)

A correlation matrix is computed to examine the pairwise relationships between indicators before running the PCA.

cor_matrix_economic <- cor(economic_indicators)  
print(cor_matrix_economic)
                            X805_Eco_Individual.income X852_Eco_Individual.income
X805_Eco_Individual.income                   1.0000000                 0.70771537
X852_Eco_Individual.income                   0.7077154                 1.00000000
X582_Eco_Occupation                          0.5881327                 0.67897759
X822_Eco_Occupation                          0.8566910                 0.66075866
X1014_Eco_Occupation                         0.1370485                 0.35425251
X283_Eco_Occupation                          0.2416702                -0.03390022
X43_Eco_Tourism.development                 -0.2711167                -0.02383313
X279_Eco_                                    0.1974709                 0.01005414
X523_Eco_                                    0.3354267                 0.01148944
                            X582_Eco_Occupation X822_Eco_Occupation X1014_Eco_Occupation
X805_Eco_Individual.income           0.58813267          0.85669101           0.13704851
X852_Eco_Individual.income           0.67897759          0.66075866           0.35425251
X582_Eco_Occupation                  1.00000000          0.70740661          -0.02550584
X822_Eco_Occupation                  0.70740661          1.00000000          -0.05428216
X1014_Eco_Occupation                -0.02550584         -0.05428216           1.00000000
X283_Eco_Occupation                 -0.09843467          0.34535283           0.11497176
X43_Eco_Tourism.development         -0.07846059         -0.34274425           0.32345229
X279_Eco_                           -0.15989950          0.25395450          -0.17542619
X523_Eco_                            0.09683509          0.41204898          -0.60826869
                            X283_Eco_Occupation X43_Eco_Tourism.development   X279_Eco_
X805_Eco_Individual.income           0.24167016                 -0.27111669  0.19747093
X852_Eco_Individual.income          -0.03390022                 -0.02383313  0.01005414
X582_Eco_Occupation                 -0.09843467                 -0.07846059 -0.15989950
X822_Eco_Occupation                  0.34535283                 -0.34274425  0.25395450
X1014_Eco_Occupation                 0.11497176                  0.32345229 -0.17542619
X283_Eco_Occupation                  1.00000000                 -0.16910038  0.42353833
X43_Eco_Tourism.development         -0.16910038                  1.00000000 -0.50861097
X279_Eco_                            0.42353833                 -0.50861097  1.00000000
X523_Eco_                            0.10996088                 -0.82170861  0.61407061
                              X523_Eco_
X805_Eco_Individual.income   0.33542670
X852_Eco_Individual.income   0.01148944
X582_Eco_Occupation          0.09683509
X822_Eco_Occupation          0.41204898
X1014_Eco_Occupation        -0.60826869
X283_Eco_Occupation          0.10996088
X43_Eco_Tourism.development -0.82170861
X279_Eco_                    0.61407061
X523_Eco_                    1.00000000

The correlation matrix is visualised as a heat map. Positive correlations appear in green, negative in red, and near-zero correlations in off-white.

ggcorrplot(cor_matrix_economic, 
           method = "circle",            
           type = "lower", 
           lab = TRUE, 
           colors = colors,
           title = "Correlation matrix - Economic dimension indicators") +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "Verdana") +
  theme(text = element_text(family = "Verdana")) +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 11),  
        axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, lineheight = 1.2),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 11)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +  
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank())

The plot is saved as a high-resolution PNG file for inclusion in the dissertation result section.

ggsave(here("1_PCA", "Results", "Economic", "heatmap_correlations_economic.png"),
       plot = last_plot(), width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)

3.1.1 Economic PCA

The PCA is computed on the scaled indicator matrix. The rotation matrix shows the contribution of each indicator to each principal component and is also saved to CSV.

#calculate Principal Components
economic_dim_results <- prcomp(economic_indicators, scale = TRUE)
  
#display Principal Components
economic_dim_results$rotation
                                    PC1        PC2         PC3         PC4         PC5
X805_Eco_Individual.income   0.45695985  0.2074900 -0.07558085 -0.01654801 -0.03851813
X852_Eco_Individual.income   0.34352752  0.4054179  0.02048091 -0.31233981  0.23959332
X582_Eco_Occupation          0.34054831  0.3313402  0.33531671  0.23004113 -0.01099525
X822_Eco_Occupation          0.49243964  0.1420912 -0.02276854  0.26026293 -0.00751954
X1014_Eco_Occupation        -0.07483474  0.4075360 -0.49604804 -0.52168193 -0.27209769
X283_Eco_Occupation          0.16865507 -0.1182357 -0.68337344  0.54323989 -0.20330606
X43_Eco_Tourism.development -0.31525168  0.3834841 -0.07147435  0.35209127  0.63306760
X279_Eco_                    0.24786103 -0.3790824 -0.35298060 -0.27262499  0.65158128
X523_Eco_                    0.34618362 -0.4379260  0.19530889 -0.12633304 -0.01518865
                                    PC6         PC7          PC8         PC9
X805_Eco_Individual.income   0.65424442  0.28386169 -0.385778876 -0.28849105
X852_Eco_Individual.income  -0.09514990 -0.73968302 -0.070468452 -0.03308018
X582_Eco_Occupation         -0.60816891  0.35394082 -0.326494317  0.08379354
X822_Eco_Occupation          0.07909725  0.10328184  0.803456979  0.08083883
X1014_Eco_Occupation        -0.09463263  0.29700923  0.060311826  0.36776891
X283_Eco_Occupation         -0.15141350 -0.26179286 -0.238724312  0.07538416
X43_Eco_Tourism.development  0.24367301  0.07473379 -0.058009533  0.39418026
X279_Eco_                   -0.25513161  0.26415158  0.009894136 -0.19083380
X523_Eco_                    0.17445597 -0.06510872 -0.173139788  0.75457934
  
# Save rotation matrix to CSV
write.csv(economic_dim_results$rotation,
          file = here("1_PCA", "Results", "Economic", "economic_pca.csv"),
          row.names = TRUE)

The variance explained by each principal component is calculated by squaring the standard deviations returned by “prcomp”, dividing by their total, and multiplying by 100 to express as a percentage.

# Calculate variance for each Principal Component
var_exp_economic_dim <- economic_dim_results$sdev^2 / sum(economic_dim_results$sdev^2)

# Show variance in percentage
var_exp_economic_dim * 100
[1] 38.9840528 27.4481838 14.8962477  7.5307708  4.7158031  3.2182024  1.7426349  0.8404219
[9]  0.6236827
# Open PNG so save graph
png(here("1_PCA", "Results", "Economic", "scree_plot_pca_economic_dimension.png"),
    width = 800, height = 600, res = 150)

par(family = "Verdana")

# Display explained variance
plot(var_exp_economic_dim * 100, type = "b", xlab = "Principal Component", 
     ylab = "Variance explained (%)", main = "Scree plot PCA Economic dimension",
     col = "black", lwd = 1, pch = 16)  

# Highlight PC1 and PC2
points(1:2, var_exp_economic_dim[1:2] * 100, col = col, pch = 16, cex = 1.5) 
lines(1:2, var_exp_economic_dim[1:2] * 100, col = col, lwd = 3)  

# Close graph
dev.off()
null device 
          1

3.1.2 Economic weight

Because PC1 and PC2 together explain a substantial and roughly comparable share of the variance (66.43%), indicator weights are derived from a combination of both components, each scaled by its proportion of explained variance. The resulting weights are normalised to sum to one.

weight_PC1_economicdim <- abs(economic_dim_results$rotation[, 1]) # Taking absolute values for PC1
normalised_weight_economicdim<- weight_PC1_economicdim / sum(weight_PC1_economicdim)  # Normalise so that they sum 1
normalised_weight_economicdim
 X805_Eco_Individual.income  X852_Eco_Individual.income         X582_Eco_Occupation 
                 0.16400466                  0.12329335                  0.12222410 
        X822_Eco_Occupation        X1014_Eco_Occupation         X283_Eco_Occupation 
                 0.17673849                  0.02685848                  0.06053096 
X43_Eco_Tourism.development                   X279_Eco_                   X523_Eco_ 
                 0.11314505                  0.08895828                  0.12424664 
# Calculate combined weights of PC1 and Pc2

# Absolute values for PC1
weight_PC1_economicdim <- abs(economic_dim_results$rotation[, 1]) 
# Absolute values for PC2
weight_PC2_economicdim <- abs(economic_dim_results$rotation[, 2]) 

# Ponderated according to explicit variance
variance_PC1 <- 0.3898  # 38.98% expressed as decimal
variance_PC2 <- 0.2745  # 27.45% expressed as decimal

# Calculation of the weighted contribution for each indicator
combined_weight_economicdim <- (weight_PC1_economicdim * variance_PC1 + 
                                weight_PC2_economicdim * variance_PC2)

# Normalise weights so they sum 1
normalised_combined_weight_economicdim <- combined_weight_economicdim / sum(combined_weight_economicdim)

# Show normalised weights
normalised_combined_weight_economicdim
 X805_Eco_Individual.income  X852_Eco_Individual.income         X582_Eco_Occupation 
                 0.12651275                  0.13195651                  0.12038818 
        X822_Eco_Occupation        X1014_Eco_Occupation         X283_Eco_Occupation 
                 0.12429443                  0.07590326                  0.05284704 
X43_Eco_Tourism.development                   X279_Eco_                   X523_Eco_ 
                 0.12278459                  0.10799717                  0.13731606 
# Save to CSV
write.csv(normalised_combined_weight_economicdim,
          file = here("1_PCA", "Results", "Economic", "economic_weight.csv"),
          row.names = TRUE)

3.2 Healthcare Dimension

healthcare_indicators <- inverted_indicators[, c("X10_Healthcare" ,"X642_Healthcare", 
"X257_Healthcare", 
"X902_Healthcare"  
)]

summary(healthcare_indicators)
 X10_Healthcare  X642_Healthcare  X257_Healthcare X902_Healthcare 
 Min.   :77.94   Min.   : 0.000   Min.   : 0.00   Min.   : 0.600  
 1st Qu.:83.95   1st Qu.: 0.000   1st Qu.: 7.00   1st Qu.: 2.300  
 Median :84.88   Median : 1.000   Median :13.00   Median : 3.100  
 Mean   :84.38   Mean   : 1.918   Mean   :15.53   Mean   : 3.677  
 3rd Qu.:85.36   3rd Qu.: 3.000   3rd Qu.:20.00   3rd Qu.: 4.400  
 Max.   :87.62   Max.   :10.000   Max.   :63.00   Max.   :10.300  
cor_matrix_healthcare <- cor(healthcare_indicators)  
print(cor_matrix_healthcare)
                X10_Healthcare X642_Healthcare X257_Healthcare X902_Healthcare
X10_Healthcare       1.0000000       0.1671926       0.2763579      -0.6416438
X642_Healthcare      0.1671926       1.0000000       0.5622868      -0.2854516
X257_Healthcare      0.2763579       0.5622868       1.0000000      -0.3744797
X902_Healthcare     -0.6416438      -0.2854516      -0.3744797       1.0000000
ggcorrplot(cor_matrix_healthcare, 
           method = "circle", 
           type = "lower", 

           lab = TRUE, 
           colors = colors,
           title = "Correlation matrix - Healthcare dimension indicators") +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "Verdana") +
  theme(text = element_text(family = "Verdana")) +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 11),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, lineheight = 1.2),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 11)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) + 
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank()) 

ggsave(here("1_PCA", "Results", "Healthcare", "heatmap_correlations_healthcare.png"),
       plot = last_plot(), width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)

3.2.1 Healthcare PCA

#calculate principal components
healthcare_dim_results <- prcomp(healthcare_indicators, scale = TRUE)
  
#display principal components
healthcare_dim_results$rotation
                       PC1        PC2         PC3         PC4
X10_Healthcare   0.4878649  0.5553751  0.03686111 -0.67244895
X642_Healthcare  0.4515653 -0.5875689  0.66037788 -0.12146051
X257_Healthcare  0.5106346 -0.4346568 -0.74126657 -0.02914833
X902_Healthcare -0.5452787 -0.3967301 -0.11430739 -0.72952734
write.csv(healthcare_dim_results$rotation,
          file = here("1_PCA", "Results", "Healthcare", "healthcare_pca.csv"),
          row.names = TRUE)
var_exp_healthcare_dim <- healthcare_dim_results$sdev^2 / sum(healthcare_dim_results$sdev^2)

var_exp_healthcare_dim * 100
[1] 54.02909 26.62959 10.68955  8.65177
png(here("1_PCA", "Results", "Healthcare", "scree_plot_pca_healthcare_dimension.png"),
    width = 800, height = 600, res = 150)

par(family = "Verdana")

plot(var_exp_healthcare_dim * 100, type = "b", xlab = "Principal Component", 
     ylab = "Variance explained (%)", main = "Scree plot PCA Healthcare dimension",
     col = "black", lwd = 1, pch = 16)

points(1:2, var_exp_healthcare_dim[1:2] * 100, col = col, pch = 16, cex = 1.5)
lines(1:2, var_exp_healthcare_dim[1:2] * 100, col = col, lwd = 3)

dev.off()
null device 
          1

3.2.2 Healthcare weight

Weights are derived from PC1 loadings only and normalised to sum to one.

pc1_weights <- abs(healthcare_dim_results$rotation[, 1]) 

normalised_pc1_weights_healthcare <- pc1_weights / sum(pc1_weights)

normalised_pc1_weights_healthcare
 X10_Healthcare X642_Healthcare X257_Healthcare X902_Healthcare 
      0.2445017       0.2263095       0.2559131       0.2732756 
write.csv(normalised_pc1_weights_healthcare,
          file = here("1_PCA", "Results", "Healthcare", "healthcare_pc1_weights.csv"),
          row.names = TRUE)

3.3 Housing and shelter dimension

The Housing and shelter dimension subset includes six indicators (one neutral indicator, X148_Housing, was removed in section 2.2).

housing_indicators <- inverted_indicators[, c("X465_Housing", "X674_Housing","X477_Housing", "X139_Housing","X866_Housing", 
"X653_Housing")]

summary(housing_indicators)
  X465_Housing    X674_Housing     X477_Housing     X139_Housing     X866_Housing  
 Min.   :36.79   Min.   :-21.06   Min.   : 52.00   Min.   :-2.930   Min.   :-5591  
 1st Qu.:58.99   1st Qu.:-16.69   1st Qu.: 64.09   1st Qu.:-2.530   1st Qu.:-4052  
 Median :63.81   Median :-15.46   Median : 68.81   Median :-2.440   Median :-3170  
 Mean   :61.94   Mean   :-14.80   Mean   : 70.89   Mean   :-2.486   Mean   :-3306  
 3rd Qu.:66.97   3rd Qu.:-13.61   3rd Qu.: 74.07   3rd Qu.:-2.390   3rd Qu.:-2489  
 Max.   :83.01   Max.   :  0.00   Max.   :115.03   Max.   :-2.050   Max.   :-1115  
  X653_Housing   
 Min.   :  0.00  
 1st Qu.: 87.72  
 Median : 92.65  
 Mean   : 89.32  
 3rd Qu.: 95.67  
 Max.   :100.00  
cor_matrix_housing <- cor(housing_indicators)  
print(cor_matrix_housing)
             X465_Housing X674_Housing X477_Housing X139_Housing X866_Housing X653_Housing
X465_Housing   1.00000000   0.04616703  -0.02402508   -0.2493474  0.335871654  0.554294139
X674_Housing   0.04616703   1.00000000  -0.41289640   -0.2845681  0.723483589  0.007069750
X477_Housing  -0.02402508  -0.41289640   1.00000000   -0.1854009 -0.673965812  0.213137957
X139_Housing  -0.24934744  -0.28456814  -0.18540085    1.0000000 -0.290630277 -0.138474237
X866_Housing   0.33587165   0.72348359  -0.67396581   -0.2906303  1.000000000  0.008009163
X653_Housing   0.55429414   0.00706975   0.21313796   -0.1384742  0.008009163  1.000000000
ggcorrplot(cor_matrix_housing, 
           method = "circle", 
           type = "lower", 

           lab = TRUE, 
           colors = colors,
           title = "Correlation matrix - Housing and shelter dimension indicators") +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "Verdana") +
  theme(text = element_text(family = "Verdana")) +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 11),  
        axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, lineheight = 1.2),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 11)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank()) 

ggsave(here("1_PCA", "Results", "Housing", "heatmap_correlations_housing.png"),
       plot = last_plot(), width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)

3.3.1 Housing PCA

housing_dim_results <- prcomp(housing_indicators, scale = TRUE)
  
#display principal components
housing_dim_results$rotation
                     PC1         PC2         PC3        PC4         PC5         PC6
X465_Housing  0.25635299  0.56262101  0.34675420 -0.4586997  0.46179231  0.27174378
X674_Housing  0.53403661 -0.11096730 -0.24248329  0.5940191  0.39445055  0.36775418
X477_Housing -0.43948891  0.38967409 -0.40489945  0.2099541  0.52522220 -0.41366769
X139_Housing -0.24299636 -0.35134690  0.74432116  0.2951370  0.38956300 -0.15693622
X866_Housing  0.62633713 -0.06623639  0.03496933 -0.1154041  0.04293615 -0.76611360
X653_Housing  0.06776509  0.62567249  0.31903445  0.5405842 -0.44910514 -0.09073141
  
write.csv(housing_dim_results$rotation,
          file = here("1_PCA", "Results", "Housing", "housing_pca.csv"),
          row.names = TRUE)
var_exp_housing_dim <- housing_dim_results$sdev^2 / sum(housing_dim_results$sdev^2)

var_exp_housing_dim * 100
[1] 39.014423 28.447307 16.739849  9.048396  4.898759  1.851267
png(here("1_PCA", "Results", "Housing", "scree_plot_pca_housing_dimension.png"),
    width = 800, height = 600, res = 150)

par(family = "Verdana")

plot(var_exp_housing_dim * 100, type = "b", xlab = "Principal Component", 
     ylab = "Variance explained (%)", main = "Scree plot PCA Housing and shelter dimension",
     col = "black", lwd = 1, pch = 16)

points(1:2, var_exp_housing_dim[1:2] * 100, col = col, pch = 16, cex = 1.5)
lines(1:2, var_exp_housing_dim[1:2] * 100, col = col, lwd = 3)

dev.off()
null device 
          1

3.3.2 Housing weight

As with the economic dimension, PC1 and PC2 are combined for weighting.

weight_PC1_housingdim <- abs(housing_dim_results$rotation[, 1]) 
normalised_weight_housingdim<- weight_PC1_housingdim / sum(weight_PC1_housingdim)
normalised_weight_housingdim
X465_Housing X674_Housing X477_Housing X139_Housing X866_Housing X653_Housing 
  0.11829981   0.24644312   0.20281198   0.11213610   0.28903727   0.03127172 
weight_PC1_housingdim <- abs(housing_dim_results$rotation[, 1])
weight_PC2_housingdim <- abs(housing_dim_results$rotation[, 2])

variance_PC1 <- 0.38961195
variance_PC2 <- 0.27828235  

combined_weight_housingdim <- (weight_PC1_housingdim * variance_PC1 + 
weight_PC2_housingdim * variance_PC2)

#Normalise weights so that they sum 1
normalised_combined_weight_housingdim <- combined_weight_housingdim / sum(combined_weight_housingdim)

normalised_combined_weight_housingdim
X465_Housing X674_Housing X477_Housing X139_Housing X866_Housing X653_Housing 
   0.1792716    0.1670391    0.1955065    0.1345332    0.1834766    0.1401730 
write.csv(normalised_combined_weight_housingdim,
          file = here("1_PCA", "Results", "Housing", "housing_weight.csv"),
          row.names = TRUE)

3.4 Infrastructures and services Dimension

The infrastructures subset includes ten indicators (one neutral indicator, X30_Inf_Water.consumption, was removed in section 2.2). Weights are based on PC1 only.

infrastructures_indicators <- inverted_indicators[, c("X877_Inf_Air.quality",
"X671_Inf_Education", 
"X809_Inf_Education",
"X744_Inf_Education", 
"X974_Inf_Education",
"X569_Inf_Leisure.and.cultural.activities",
"X198_Inf_Leisure.and.cultural.activities",
"X484_Inf_Nature.based.assets", 
"X633_Inf_Nature.based.assets",
"X1041_Inf_Security.and.safety"
)]

summary(infrastructures_indicators)
 X877_Inf_Air.quality X671_Inf_Education X809_Inf_Education X744_Inf_Education
 Min.   :  258        Min.   :50.87      Min.   :-24.30     Min.   :     0

cor_matrix_infrastructures <- cor(infrastructures_indicators)

X1041_Inf_Security.and.safety                     0.204654888         0.20254424
                                         X809_Inf_Education X744_Inf_Education
X877_Inf_Air.quality                             0.51815810         0.43183671
X671_Inf_Education                               0.86148259         0.28252349
X809_Inf_Education                               1.00000000         0.22304667
X744_Inf_Education                               0.22304667         1.00000000
X974_Inf_Education                               0.89538855         0.25933132
X569_Inf_Leisure.and.cultural.activities         0.14598797         0.09292218
X198_Inf_Leisure.and.cultural.activities         0.26891487         0.11905560
X484_Inf_Nature.based.assets                     0.07440122        -0.06700120
X633_Inf_Nature.based.assets                    -0.15224416         0.01921342
X1041_Inf_Security.and.safety                    0.22338286         0.09630013

X877_Inf_Air.quality                                       0.204654888

ggcorrplot(cor_matrix_infrastructures, 
           method = "circle", 
           type = "lower", 

           lab = TRUE, 
           colors = colors,
           title = "Correlation matrix - Infrastructures and services dimension indicators") +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "Verdana") +
  theme(text = element_text(family = "Verdana")) +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 11),  
        axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, lineheight = 1.2),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 11)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) + 
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank()) 

ggsave(here("1_PCA", "Results", "Infrastructures", "heatmap_correlations_infrastructures.png"),
       plot = last_plot(), width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)

3.4.1 Infrastructures PCA

The variance explained by each component is calculated as follows: the standard deviations returned by “prcomp” are squared to obtain variances, divided by their total to obtain proportions, and multiplied by 100 to express as percentages.

#calculate principal components
infrastructures_dim_results <- prcomp(infrastructures_indicators, scale = TRUE)
  
#display principal components
infrastructures_dim_results$rotation
                                                 PC1         PC2         PC3         PC4
X877_Inf_Air.quality                      0.39058680 -0.02372510 -0.18504505  0.32615604
X671_Inf_Education                        0.49078184  0.06045968  0.09663104 -0.10289929
X809_Inf_Education                        0.45552432  0.16061759  0.23721674 -0.17116429
X744_Inf_Education                        0.21798942  0.07637872 -0.17602773  0.62367587
X974_Inf_Education                        0.45893331  0.17015066  0.28891664 -0.08403746
X569_Inf_Leisure.and.cultural.activities  0.18366048 -0.64037623 -0.14876366 -0.09674407
X198_Inf_Leisure.and.cultural.activities  0.28530542 -0.42805465 -0.43619179 -0.06132966
X484_Inf_Nature.based.assets              0.02144996 -0.44145471  0.59650237 -0.16314411
X633_Inf_Nature.based.assets             -0.07205101 -0.19615316  0.46504571  0.64156802
X1041_Inf_Security.and.safety             0.14195691  0.32943583 -0.03484115 -0.07972838
                                                 PC5          PC6         PC7         PC8
X877_Inf_Air.quality                     -0.06880555 -0.099129964  0.53738146  0.63106540
X671_Inf_Education                        0.14562795 -0.071201700 -0.12227268 -0.16107104
X809_Inf_Education                        0.11833047  0.016908105 -0.07456463  0.04822435
X744_Inf_Education                       -0.13985682  0.634001145 -0.17143331 -0.26977726
X974_Inf_Education                        0.19006124 -0.009953099 -0.13922884 -0.04780285
X569_Inf_Leisure.and.cultural.activities -0.19324164  0.015649776 -0.59688146  0.35883265
X198_Inf_Leisure.and.cultural.activities  0.04787496 -0.270378179  0.30931155 -0.57614061
X484_Inf_Nature.based.assets             -0.31818308  0.374689496  0.40779342 -0.09988655
X633_Inf_Nature.based.assets              0.03679391 -0.537515014 -0.13105689 -0.11868294
X1041_Inf_Security.and.safety            -0.87300881 -0.283014636 -0.08077208 -0.11687721
                                                  PC9         PC10
X877_Inf_Air.quality                      0.068374908  0.001088261
X671_Inf_Education                        0.449815853  0.682675633
X809_Inf_Education                       -0.805081056  0.103930876
X744_Inf_Education                       -0.054330781 -0.007155048
X974_Inf_Education                        0.343643956 -0.702787706
X569_Inf_Leisure.and.cultural.activities -0.004267771 -0.045423265
X198_Inf_Leisure.and.cultural.activities -0.121115304 -0.153845730
X484_Inf_Nature.based.assets              0.056250373  0.034014164
X633_Inf_Nature.based.assets             -0.075671223  0.034520903
X1041_Inf_Security.and.safety             0.014652379 -0.033301292
  
write.csv(infrastructures_dim_results$rotation,
          file = here("1_PCA", "Results", "Infrastructures", "infrastructures_pca.csv"),
          row.names = TRUE)
var_exp_infrastructures_dim <- infrastructures_dim_results$sdev^2 / sum(infrastructures_dim_results$sdev^2)

var_exp_infrastructures_dim * 100
 [1] 37.0576607 14.4322554 11.4153536 10.8192063  9.0840154  7.5527445  4.8364158
 [8]  3.2063205  1.2518120  0.3442158
png(here("1_PCA", "Results", "Infrastructures", "scree_plot_pca_infrastructures_dimension.png"),
    width = 800, height = 600, res = 150)

par(family = "Verdana")

plot(var_exp_infrastructures_dim * 100, type = "b", xlab = "Principal Component", 
     ylab = "Variance explained (%)", main = "Scree plot PCA Infrastructures and services dimension",
     col = "black", lwd = 1, pch = 16)

points(1:2, var_exp_infrastructures_dim[1:2] * 100, col = col, pch = 16, cex = 1.5)
lines(1:2, var_exp_infrastructures_dim[1:2] * 100, col = col, lwd = 3)

dev.off()
null device 
          1

3.4.2 Infrastructures weight

pc1_weights <- abs(infrastructures_dim_results$rotation[, 1])

normalised_pc1_weights_infrastructures <- pc1_weights / sum(pc1_weights)

normalised_pc1_weights_infrastructures
                    X877_Inf_Air.quality                       X671_Inf_Education 
                             0.143691093                              0.180551363 
                      X809_Inf_Education                       X744_Inf_Education 
                             0.167580644                              0.080195075 
                      X974_Inf_Education X569_Inf_Leisure.and.cultural.activities 
                             0.168834762                              0.067565967 
X198_Inf_Leisure.and.cultural.activities             X484_Inf_Nature.based.assets 
                             0.104959635                              0.007891121 
            X633_Inf_Nature.based.assets            X1041_Inf_Security.and.safety 
                             0.026506497                              0.052223841 
write.csv(normalised_pc1_weights_infrastructures,
          file = here("1_PCA", "Results", "Infrastructures", "infrastructures_pc1_weights.csv"),
          row.names = TRUE)

3.5 Mobility and accessibility dimension

The Mobility and accessibility dimension includes three indicators. With only three indicators, the PCA produces three components; PC1 is used for weighting.

mobility_indicators <- inverted_indicators[, c("X989_Mob_", "X361_Mob_", "X515_Mob_")]

summary(mobility_indicators)
   X989_Mob_        X361_Mob_         X515_Mob_   
 Min.   :-66.67   Min.   :-8891.5   Min.   :  30  
 1st Qu.:-22.22   1st Qu.: -548.2   1st Qu.: 623  
 Median :-14.67   Median : -463.1   Median :1289  
 Mean   :-18.58   Mean   : -605.9   Mean   :1461  
 3rd Qu.: -9.77   3rd Qu.: -421.3   3rd Qu.:2020  
 Max.   : -3.70   Max.   : -262.1   Max.   :5249  
cor_matrix_mobility <- cor(mobility_indicators)  
print(cor_matrix_mobility)
          X989_Mob_ X361_Mob_ X515_Mob_
X989_Mob_ 1.0000000 0.3715030 0.4041864
X361_Mob_ 0.3715030 1.0000000 0.1127538
X515_Mob_ 0.4041864 0.1127538 1.0000000
ggcorrplot(cor_matrix_mobility, 
           method = "circle", 
           type = "lower", 

           lab = TRUE, 
           colors = colors,
           title = "Correlation matrix - Mobility and accessibility dimension indicators") +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "Verdana") +
  theme(text = element_text(family = "Verdana")) +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 11),  
        axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, lineheight = 1.2),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 11)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) + 
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank()) 

ggsave(here("1_PCA", "Results", "Mobility", "heatmap_correlations_mobility.png"),
       plot = last_plot(), width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)

3.5.1 Mobility PCA

#calculate principal components
mobility_dim_results <- prcomp(mobility_indicators, scale = TRUE)
  
#display principal components
mobility_dim_results$rotation
                PC1         PC2        PC3
X989_Mob_ 0.6700762 -0.01885247 -0.7420529
X361_Mob_ 0.5094694  0.73872103  0.4412847
X515_Mob_ 0.5398507 -0.67374759  0.5046042
write.csv(mobility_dim_results$rotation,
          file = here("1_PCA", "Results", "Mobility", "mobility_pca.csv"),
          row.names = TRUE)
var_exp_mobility_dim <- mobility_dim_results$sdev^2 / sum(mobility_dim_results$sdev^2)

var_exp_mobility_dim * 100
[1] 53.60315 29.58941 16.80743
png(here("1_PCA", "Results", "Mobility", "scree_plot_pca_mobility_dimension.png"),
    width = 800, height = 600, res = 150)

par(family = "Verdana")

plot(var_exp_mobility_dim * 100, type = "b", xlab = "Principal Component", 
     ylab = "Variance explained (%)", main = "Scree plot PCA Mobility and accessibility dimension",
     col = "black", lwd = 1, pch = 16)

points(1:2, var_exp_mobility_dim[1:2] * 100, col = col, pch = 16, cex = 1.5)
lines(1:2, var_exp_mobility_dim[1:2] * 100, col = col, lwd = 3)

dev.off()
null device 
          1

3.5.2 Mobility weight

pc1_weights <- abs(mobility_dim_results$rotation[, 1])

normalised_pc1_weights_mobility <- pc1_weights / sum(pc1_weights)

normalised_pc1_weights_mobility
X989_Mob_ X361_Mob_ X515_Mob_ 
0.3897160 0.2963071 0.3139769 
write.csv(normalised_pc1_weights_mobility,
          file = here("1_PCA", "Results", "Mobility", "mobility_pc1_weights.csv"),
          row.names = TRUE)

3.6 Social inclusion Dimension

Following the removal of five neutral indicators in section 2.2, the social inclusion dimension retains three indicators: cultural diversity and identity (X329), public participation and trust in institutions (X799), and social participation (X565). Weights are based on PC1 only.

social_indicators <- inverted_indicators[, c(
"X329_Soc_Cultural.diversity.and.identity",
"X799_Soc_Public.participation.and.trust.in.institutions",
"X565_Soc_Social.participation"
)]

summary(social_indicators)
 X329_Soc_Cultural.diversity.and.identity
 Min.   : 0.00                           
 1st Qu.:18.83                           
 Median :35.71                           
 Mean   :32.68                           
 3rd Qu.:45.36                           
 Max.   :66.67                           
 X799_Soc_Public.participation.and.trust.in.institutions X565_Soc_Social.participation
 Min.   :-16.44                                          Min.   :  3.00               
 1st Qu.:-12.07                                          1st Qu.: 26.00               
 Median : -9.59                                          Median : 50.00               
 Mean   :-10.65                                          Mean   : 75.97               
 3rd Qu.: -8.71                                          3rd Qu.: 84.00               
 Max.   : -6.65                                          Max.   :360.00               
cor_matrix_social <- cor(social_indicators)  
print(cor_matrix_social)
                                                        X329_Soc_Cultural.diversity.and.identity
X329_Soc_Cultural.diversity.and.identity                                               1.0000000
X799_Soc_Public.participation.and.trust.in.institutions                                0.7344362
X565_Soc_Social.participation                                                          0.1957832
                                                        X799_Soc_Public.participation.and.trust.in.institutions
X329_Soc_Cultural.diversity.and.identity                                                             0.73443620
X799_Soc_Public.participation.and.trust.in.institutions                                              1.00000000
X565_Soc_Social.participation                                                                        0.09660932
                                                        X565_Soc_Social.participation
X329_Soc_Cultural.diversity.and.identity                                   0.19578315
X799_Soc_Public.participation.and.trust.in.institutions                    0.09660932
X565_Soc_Social.participation                                              1.00000000
ggcorrplot(cor_matrix_social, 
           method = "circle", 
           type = "lower", 

           lab = TRUE, 
           colors = colors,
           title = "Correlation matrix - Social cohesion dimension indicators") +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "Verdana") +
  theme(text = element_text(family = "Verdana")) +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 11),  
        axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, lineheight = 1.2),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 11)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) + 
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank()) 

ggsave(here("1_PCA", "Results", "Social", "heatmap_correlations_social.png"),
       plot = last_plot(), width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)

3.6.1 Social inclusion PCA

social_dim_results <- prcomp(social_indicators, scale = TRUE)

social_dim_results$rotation
                                                              PC1        PC2         PC3
X329_Soc_Cultural.diversity.and.identity                0.6920600 -0.1104286 -0.71334318
X799_Soc_Public.participation.and.trust.in.institutions 0.6754915 -0.2493301  0.69393500
X565_Soc_Social.participation                           0.2544882  0.9621019  0.09795781
  
write.csv(social_dim_results$rotation,
          file = here("1_PCA", "Results", "Social", "social_pca.csv"),
          row.names = TRUE)
var_exp_social_dim <- social_dim_results$sdev^2 / sum(social_dim_results$sdev^2)

var_exp_social_dim * 100
[1] 59.628255 31.749728  8.622017
png(here("1_PCA", "Results", "Social", "scree_plot_pca_social_dimension.png"),
    width = 800, height = 600, res = 150)

par(family = "Verdana")

plot(var_exp_social_dim * 100, type = "b", xlab = "Principal Component", 
     ylab = "Variance explained (%)", main = "Scree plot PCA Social cohesion dimension",
     col = "black", lwd = 1, pch = 16)

points(1:2, var_exp_social_dim[1:2] * 100, col = col, pch = 16, cex = 1.5)
lines(1:2, var_exp_social_dim[1:2] * 100, col = col, lwd = 3)

dev.off()
null device 
          1

3.6.2 Social inclusion weight

pc1_weights <- abs(social_dim_results$rotation[, 1])

normalised_pc1_weights_social <- pc1_weights / sum(pc1_weights)

normalised_pc1_weights_social
               X329_Soc_Cultural.diversity.and.identity 
                                              0.4266603 
X799_Soc_Public.participation.and.trust.in.institutions 
                                              0.4164457 
                          X565_Soc_Social.participation 
                                              0.1568940 
write.csv(normalised_pc1_weights_social,
          file = here("1_PCA", "Results", "Social", "social_pc1_weights.csv"),
          row.names = TRUE)

3.7 Urban environment dimension

The Urban environment dimension includes four indicators. As with the economic and Housing and shelter dimensions, PC1 and PC2 are combined for weighting.

urban_indicators <- inverted_indicators[, c("X348_Urb_Population.density", 
"X265_Urb_", 
"X534_Urb_" ,
"X479_Inf_Noise"
)]

summary(urban_indicators)
 X348_Urb_Population.density   X265_Urb_      X534_Urb_     X479_Inf_Noise  
 Min.   :-1384.64            Min.   :  45   Min.   :17.15   Min.   :-92.51  
 1st Qu.: -905.99            1st Qu.: 649   1st Qu.:44.75   1st Qu.:-77.48  
 Median : -728.60            Median :1008   Median :51.67   Median :-71.10  
 Mean   : -700.22            Mean   :1330   Mean   :51.23   Mean   :-71.74  
 3rd Qu.: -564.00            3rd Qu.:1971   3rd Qu.:58.99   3rd Qu.:-64.47  
 Max.   :  -19.67            Max.   :4833   Max.   :83.92   Max.   :-45.30  
cor_matrix_urban <- cor(urban_indicators)  
print(cor_matrix_urban)
                            X348_Urb_Population.density    X265_Urb_   X534_Urb_
X348_Urb_Population.density                 1.000000000 -0.008067398 -0.10383509
X265_Urb_                                  -0.008067398  1.000000000  0.33106392
X534_Urb_                                  -0.103835093  0.331063916  1.00000000
X479_Inf_Noise                             -0.095785689  0.360430319  0.08237983
                            X479_Inf_Noise
X348_Urb_Population.density    -0.09578569
X265_Urb_                       0.36043032
X534_Urb_                       0.08237983
X479_Inf_Noise                  1.00000000
ggcorrplot(cor_matrix_urban, 
           method = "circle",
           type = "lower", 

           lab = TRUE, 
           colors = colors,
           title = "Correlation matrix - Urban environment dimension indicators") +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "Verdana") +
  theme(text = element_text(family = "Verdana")) +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 11), 
        axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, lineheight = 1.2),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 11)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank())  

ggsave(here("1_PCA", "Results", "Urban", "heatmap_correlations_urban.png"),
       plot = last_plot(), width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)

3.7.1 Urban Environment PCA

urban_dim_results <- prcomp(urban_indicators, scale = TRUE)
  
urban_dim_results$rotation
                                   PC1         PC2         PC3        PC4
X348_Urb_Population.density  0.1960949  0.95175874 -0.13693371 -0.1922271
X265_Urb_                   -0.6507360  0.27331403 -0.02600133  0.7079308
X534_Urb_                   -0.5047390 -0.09260266 -0.72780013 -0.4549399
X479_Inf_Noise              -0.5322857  0.10430477  0.67147603 -0.5048885
  
write.csv(urban_dim_results$rotation,
          file = here("1_PCA", "Results", "Urban", "urban_pca.csv"),
          row.names = TRUE)
var_exp_urban_dim <- urban_dim_results$sdev^2 / sum(urban_dim_results$sdev^2)

var_exp_urban_dim * 100
[1] 38.85104 24.93222 22.90717 13.30957
png(here("1_PCA", "Results", "Urban", "scree_plot_pca_urban_dimension.png"),
    width = 800, height = 600, res = 150)

par(family = "Verdana")

plot(var_exp_urban_dim * 100, type = "b", xlab = "Principal Component", 
     ylab = "Variance explained (%)", main = "Scree plot PCA Urban Environment dimension",
     col = "black", lwd = 1, pch = 16)

points(1:2, var_exp_urban_dim[1:2] * 100, col = col, pch = 16, cex = 1.5)
lines(1:2, var_exp_urban_dim[1:2] * 100, col = col, lwd = 3)

dev.off()
null device 
          1

3.7.2 Urban environment weight

weight_PC1_urbandim <- abs(urban_dim_results$rotation[, 1])
weight_PC2_urbandim <- abs(urban_dim_results$rotation[, 2])

variance_PC1 <- 0.3885104   
variance_PC2 <- 0.2493222 

combined_weight_urbandim <- (weight_PC1_urbandim * variance_PC1 + 
                                weight_PC2_urbandim * variance_PC2)

normalised_combined_weight_urbandim <- combined_weight_urbandim / sum(combined_weight_urbandim)

normalised_combined_weight_urbandim
X348_Urb_Population.density                   X265_Urb_                   X534_Urb_ 
                  0.2885412                   0.2954275                   0.2017475 
             X479_Inf_Noise 
                  0.2142838 
write.csv(normalised_combined_weight_urbandim,
          file = here("1_PCA", "Results", "Urban", "urban_weight.csv"),
          row.names = TRUE)

#4. Aggregating indicators Once all dimension weights have been calculated, a composite index score is computed for each neighbourhood and dimension. Each indicator is first standardised using z-score normalisation (mean = 0, sd = 1) to allow comparison across different units and scales. The standardised values are then multiplied by their respective weights and summed to produce a single score per dimension per neighbourhood. The seven dimension scores are combined into a single summary table alongside neighbourhood identifiers.

# Extract neighbourhood IDs and names
barrio_info <- inverted_indicators %>%
  select(codi_barri, nom_barri, "Barrio.turístico.en.2023", satisfaction)

# Function to normalise, weight, and aggregate indicators
calculate_dimension_index <- function(indicators, weights) {
  indicators_scaled <- scale(indicators)
  weighted_scores <- indicators_scaled %*% weights
  return(as.numeric(weighted_scores))
}

# Calculate index by dimension
economic_index <- calculate_dimension_index(economic_indicators, normalised_combined_weight_economicdim)
healthcare_index <- calculate_dimension_index(healthcare_indicators, normalised_pc1_weights_healthcare)
housing_index <- calculate_dimension_index(housing_indicators, normalised_combined_weight_housingdim)
infrastructure_index <- calculate_dimension_index(infrastructures_indicators, normalised_pc1_weights_infrastructures)
mobility_index <- calculate_dimension_index(mobility_indicators, normalised_pc1_weights_mobility)
social_index <- calculate_dimension_index(social_indicators, normalised_pc1_weights_social)
urban_index <- calculate_dimension_index(urban_indicators, normalised_combined_weight_urbandim)

# Combine in one table
aggregated_indicators <- barrio_info %>% 
  mutate(
    Economic = economic_index,
    Healthcare = healthcare_index,
    Housing = housing_index,
    Infrastructure = infrastructure_index,
    Mobility = mobility_index,
    Social = social_index,
    Urban = urban_index
  )

The first rows of the aggregated table are displayed as a preview.

head(aggregated_indicators)

The final table is saved as a CSV file for use in subsequent analyses.

write.csv(aggregated_indicators,
          file = here("1_PCA", "Results", "Aggregated", "aggregated_indicators_by_neighbourhood.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE,
          quote = TRUE)
# Capture session information to document the computational environment
session <- sessionInfo()

# Extract package names and versions from loaded packages
pkg_info <- sapply(session$otherPkgs, function(p) {
  paste0("- ", p$Package, " (", p$Version, ")")
})

# Build README content
readme_lines <- c(
  "# UQoL Indicators in Barcelona 2025 - PCA",
  "",
  "## Overview",
  "This repository contains the R Notebook used to compute Principal Component Analysis (PCA) weights and composite UQoL dimension indices for Barcelona neighbourhoods. The analysis is part of a PhD dissertation examining the relationship between tourism impacts and urban quality of life.",
  "",
  "## Repository structure",
  "```",
  "├── UQoL_PCA_Barcelona.Rmd              # This notebook",
  "├── Indicators PCA_Neighbourhood data.csv  # Input data (required)",
  "└── Results/                            # Created automatically on first run",
  "    ├── Economic/",
  "    ├── Healthcare/",
  "    ├── Housing/",
  "    ├── Infrastructures/",
  "    ├── Mobility/",
  "    ├── Social/",
  "    ├── Urban/",
  "    └── Aggregated/",
  "```",
  "",
  "## How to run",
  "1. Place this notebook and `Indicators PCA_Neighbourhood data.csv` in the same directory.",
  "2. Open the notebook in RStudio.",
  "3. Run all chunks in order (Ctrl+Alt+R / Cmd+Alt+R).",
  "4. All outputs are saved automatically under `Results/`.",
  "",
  "## Requirements",
  paste0("- R version: ", 
         paste(session$R.version$major, session$R.version$minor, sep = ".")),
  "- The following packages are installed and loaded automatically if missing:",
  "",
  paste(sort(pkg_info), collapse = "\n"),
  "",
  "## Computational environment",
  paste0("- Platform: ", session$platform),
  paste0("- Operating system: ", session$running),
  paste0("- README generated: ", format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")),
  "",
  "## Citation",
  "If you use or adapt this code, please cite the associated dissertation:",
  "[Author, Year, Title, Institution — to be completed before submission]"
)

# Write to README.md in the project root
writeLines(readme_lines, here("1_PCA", "README.md"))

cat("README.md created successfully.\n")
README.md created successfully.
